channel.title: Comments for page "I stared into the face of everything and nothing and made it back alive"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "I stared into the face of everything and nothing and made it back alive"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh, dang. This reminds me of exactly how deep and dark that SCP community writers are capable of going. This literally takes the concept of an ordinary gay couple, and then throws that classic SCP psychological horror in. Good job, OP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
cute and gay

entertaining read, great work!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh man I wish I'd seen this right after reading 3999! That article was such a downer but this gives it a nice hopeful ending. Although my heart broke when draven found out they already tried amnesticizing him multiple times, both because if it had succeeded he would have never gotten to say goodbye and because of just how much 3999 broke James mind. Things may never be perfect for him but they are slowly getting better and I appreciate this ending for someone who went through so much.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: omg. this makes 3999 so much better to read! Upvoted heartily.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Haha thanks XD

Yeah I suppose that is true; I mean I intended it to be the reality in the first place, just that it was a bit tempting :P And I do like happy endings.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I completely forgot I crit this???

Ugh, I'm super late to the party but I just got around to upvoting this and yes, it's good and I love it. And I'm glad you didn't take the nightmare route. Because that would've been wholly expected, honestly (can you tell I consume too much horror/mindfuck media when this is what I automatically assume is going to happen).

I maintain my stance that the foundation could use more happy(ish) endings. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It would be pretty cool if it SEEMS to go the nightmare route, then comes back to see it's just Talloran being super paranoid. That'll even hit the readers' PTSD, hahaha…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The thing is, likely, this DID happen in the torture in the universe Talloran didn't survive.

…Heck, it probably did happen during the torture in the universe where Talloran lived! Word by word, all of it. Except maybe a happy ending. So then this story would sorta be a story that happened in multiple settings, but we won't know. But that feeling of ambiguity doesn't seem to be here, at least.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this exploration of possibly my favourite SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Please ignore this string of expletives.

**

I'm so glad you went the route you did. Going the "it was a nightmare" route would have been too much. It's better to either A) Leave the fear or B) My preferred is an honest exploration of PTSD.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Things are going to get better tho! Even tho he can never be certain, but maybe one day he will just no longer care about wether this is real and instead just enjoy the process.

Didn't really go down the nightmare route because it was not what I was aiming for, but I can't deny that the imagery is tempting :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Poor Talloran. Until the day he dies he'll never be truly certain, because it's all been done to him before. I wouldn't have liked, personally, if this had been another mind screw.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I would have said have Draven Stab James in the back, as that would most likely hurt more, and all the more likely to happen because of you-know-who's infinite torture… Fetish?

On the topic of the tale, i loved it. I found this to be a good display of what would happen had, well, this happened. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I sticked to the original idea, but this would indeed make an interesting alternative ending. And thanks <3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A suggestion I made was ending it with James still in the nightmare, with Draven’s face melting as they hugged.

But I do indeed enjoy this. Easy +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A tale based off SCP-3999 and Portraits Of Your Father, where Talloran survived 3999. It deals with the aftermath, with no meta proptions.

Great thanks to Perelka_L, thefriendlyvandal, v1als, LordStonefish, not_a_seagull, Avocadonut, Modern_Erasmus, weryllium, DrChandra, salvagebar, Lex1nat0r, Decibelle, minmin, taylor_itkin, Tufto, Renasanse and all other who read and/or helped with the tale.

Wrote this around two months ago, but didn't clean it up until now. A small part of this has been quoted into 3999's hidden text which I'm very proud of XD

Also, despite the subject, this is not actually related to Decibelle's ... Like Clockwork series, but I highly recommend that :P
